title: Chocolate sponge
subtitle: Foundational creamed chocolate cake
excerpt: A foundational chocolate sponge made by the creaming method with cocoa powder and a splash of hot coffee to deepen the flavour. Two 20 cm layers filled and topped with chocolate buttercream. Serves eight.
sourceNotes: Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136: a chocolate cake made by the creaming method with cocoa powder, butter, sugar, and eggs is documented in the cake chapter as a domestic preparation. Eliza Acton, Modern Cookery for Private Families (1845), Project Gutenberg: chocolate-enriched sponge cakes using grated or powdered chocolate are documented as a Victorian refinement of the plain sponge. The addition of hot coffee to deepen chocolate flavour (without imparting a coffee taste) is a widely documented technique in British domestic baking.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze the unfrosted, cooled layers individually, wrapped in cling film, for up to 2 months.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Bake the layers a day ahead and store wrapped at room temperature. Make and apply the buttercream on the day.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, middle shelf; the sponge is done when it springs back to a gentle press and a skewer through the centre comes out clean

content.text: The hot coffee trick: a tablespoon of strong instant coffee dissolved in 2 tablespoons of boiling water, stirred into the batter at the end, deepens the chocolate flavour without making the cake taste of coffee. Leave it out and the cake is still good; put it in and the chocolate is noticeably richer.

Preheat the oven to 180°C fan. Grease and line two 20 cm sandwich tins. Beat the {{unsalted-butter}} and {{caster-sugar}} until pale and light; 3 minutes with a hand mixer. Add the {{eggs}} one at a time, beating after each. Fold in the sifted {{self-raising-flour}}, {{cocoa-powder}}, {{baking-powder}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}} with a large spoon until just combined. Stir in the dissolved {{coffee-instant}} if using. Divide between the tins and bake for 25–28 minutes until a skewer comes out clean. Cool in the tins for 5 minutes, then turn out onto a rack.

Beat the remaining {{unsalted-butter}} until very pale; 3 minutes. Sift in the {{icing-sugar}} and {{cocoa-powder}} in two additions, beating after each. Add the {{double-cream}} and beat until light and fluffy. Spread a generous layer of buttercream on the flat side of the bottom sponge and sandwich with the second. Spread the remaining buttercream over the top.
